commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Telegram progress drafts to carry structured compositor lines into `updatePreview` so Telegram can render tool, reasoning, and commentary progress as rich HTML separated by `
`, with related draft-stream and compositor tests updated. PR surface: Source +101, Tests +143. Total +244 across 7 files. Reproducibility: Partly. Source inspection shows current main composes Telegram progress drafts with single-newline formatted text and sends them through the rich-message draft path, but this read-only review did not independently reproduce the visual collapse in a live Telegram client.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed for real Telegram proof and maintainer acceptance of the SDK-visible callback expansion; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue from this review.

target.title: fix(telegram): render progress drafts as rich previews
target.body:
### Summary Telegram progress drafts now render from structured progress lines into a caller-provided rich preview instead of forcing Telegram spacing through the shared plain-text formatter. This removes the PR's `lineSeparator` special case from core. Core still composes portable draft text with normal newlines; Telegram uses the same structured `ChannelProgressDraftLine` objects to build `sendRichMessage` / `editMessageText` HTML with explicit `
` breaks. ### Real behavior proof - **Behavior addressed:** streamed Telegram tool-progress entries collapsed together when rich markdown treated single newlines as soft breaks. - **Real environment tested:** live Telegram bot-to-bot run from this PR branch, using a temporary OpenClaw gateway, the Telegram plugin's real draft stream path, and Bot API rich messages. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ```bash node /Users/obviyus/.codex/skills/custom/telegram-e2e-bot-to-bot/scripts/run-mock-sut-e2e.mjs --text '@{sut} mcp code mode api file qa check. Return the OK marker from the tool result.' --expect Exec --any-sut-reply --timeout-ms 90000 --output /tmp/openclaw-telegram-rich-progress-pr93124-retry.json ``` - **Evidence after fix:** copied live terminal output from the passing Telegram run: ```text ok: true sent.messageId: 32453 sent.text: @foremanclawbot mcp code mode api file qa check. Return the OK marker from the tool result. reply.messageId: 32454 reply.fromUsername: foremanclawbot reply.replyToMessageId: 32453 reply.text excerpt: Scuttling 🛠️ Exec running 🛠️ Exec Validation failed for tool "exec": ... failed providerRequests: 2 gatewayLogTail: "" ``` - **Observed result after fix:** the real Telegram reply is threaded to the driver message and the progress rows are separated instead of being joined into one long monospace line. - **What was not tested:** broad/full CI locally. A first live attempt hit a shared Telegram `getUpdates` token conflict, then the same progress probe passed on retry. ### Verification - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/progress-draft-compositor.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts` - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-telegram-rich-progress-pr93124.tsbuildinfo` - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test-telegram-rich-progress-pr93124.tsbuildinfo` - `git diff --check` - `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test-helpers.ts src/channels/progress-draft-compositor.ts src/channels/progress-draft-compositor.test.ts src/channels/streaming.ts` - Normal Telegram reply smoke: sent `32457`, observed `32458` with `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK`, provider requests: 1.
